paragraphs.text: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews' comments on the European Commission's consultation on the 'Digital Fairness Act.'

evidence_quotes.quote: not include further regulation of reviews within the consultation

paragraphs.text: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews (CfTR) is pleased to have the opportunity to share its feedback to the consultation on the European Commission's Digital Fairness Act. The Coalition welcomes the Commission's effort to review wider gaps in consumer protection, and supports its decision to not include further regulation of reviews within the consultation, believing that the consumer reviews are sufficiently covered by existing European legislation.

By way of introduction, the Coalition for Trusted Reviews is the first global, cross-industry collaboration promoting trust in customer reviews. Our founding members are Amazon, Booking.com, Expedia Group, Glassdoor, Tripadvisor and Trustpilot, with new members joining this year: the Better Business Bureau, Docplanner, HolidayCheck and G2. We are committed to upholding the highest standards of integrity in consumer reviews and share industry best practices for maintaining the authenticity of reviews, instilling confidence in consumers worldwide. Our members appreciate the significant risks posed by fake reviews and have already made substantial progress in addressing them.

Reviews play a vital role in empowering consumers to make informed decisions by shaping consumer confidence and ensuring transparency in the market. Our business models, though different, depend on delivering accurate, trustworthy information that enables consumers to make informed choices. For that reason, the Coalition has come together to collaborate on promoting trusted reviews, to share knowledge and best practices around hosting reviews, and to work with the broader ecosystem to tackle fake reviews holistically.

Notably, our Coalition has worked together to develop best practices for hosting consumer reviews, aimed at promoting the highest standards of integrity, transparency, and accountability. These best practices are based on input taken from trust and safety practitioners, legal experts and existing fraud prevention frameworks. They were designed to raise standards across the industry and to ensure consumers can place trust in customer reviews and ratings. Please see Annex A to learn more about our best practices.
To note, the Coalition holds its own definition of a 'fake review', meaning:

evidence_quotes.quote: Reviews and ratings that are not based on a genuine experience, or otherwise deliberately mislead, with the purpose of manipulating others.

paragraphs.text: 'Reviews and ratings that are not based on a genuine experience, or otherwise deliberately mislead, with the purpose of manipulating others.'

evidence_quotes.quote: public right of reply to offer their own version of events.

paragraphs.text: It is significant to members that this definition emphasises the intention behind a review, recognising that often sometimes businesses may seek to challenge a review as 'fake' due to not recognising the experience that the consumer set out. In such cases, we do not believe that the contested review should be removed, and instead businesses are allowed a public right of reply to offer their own version of events.

The Coalition operates on a global scale, engaging previously in other jurisdictions with regulators such as the CMA and FTC, while they have introduced flexible and proportionate measures to protect consumers from fake reviews. This year, the Coalition has also provided
feedback to the European Commission on the Italian Annual Bill on Small & Medium-Sized Enterprises and its Chapter IV of Fake Reviews.

The Coalition would like to take this opportunity to reiterate its position on best practice for trusted reviews and the responsibility of platforms to consumers, particularly considering developments in individual member states that are causing a fragmented regulatory environment for companies to navigate, such as the recent Italian Annual Bill on Small & Medium-Sized Enterprises and its Chapter IV of Fake Reviews.

evidence_quotes.quote: principle of reviews not being included within the upcoming Digital Fairness Act.
the existing EU regulatory framework covering reviews is solid and efficient.

paragraphs.text: As mentioned, we welcome and agree with the principle of reviews not being included within the upcoming Digital Fairness Act. The Coalition's members agree that the existing EU regulatory framework covering reviews is solid and efficient. Specifically:

The EU Omnibus Directive (Directive 2005/29/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 May 2005 concerning unfair business-to-consumer commercial practices in the internal market, amending the EU "Unfair Commercial Practices Directive").
The Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19 October 2022 on a Single Market For Digital Services and amending Directive 2000/31/EC (Digital Services Act);

Regulation (EU) 2019/1150 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 20 June 2019 on promoting fairness and transparency for business users of online intermediation services, hereafter referred to as Platform to Business Regulation;

The recently adopted Code of Conduct for Online Ratings and Reviews for Tourism Accommodation, building on existing initiatives such as the 2023 Consumer Protection Pledge and the industry-led Coalition for Trusted Reviews.

The Coalition encourages and supports the Commission continuing to strengthen the integrity of reviews by drawing upon existing legislation, and to ensure clear and consistent enforcement led by the harmonisation principle that is threatened by the fragmentation in the European regulatory environment created by the creation of new laws, as well as the inconsistent application of existing rules, by member states. Notably, this can be found in the current developments in member states, such as the Italian Annual Bill on Small & Medium-Sized Enterprises and its Chapter IV of Fake Reviews.

The differences in the application and interpretation of the rules was also highlighted in the EU's 2024 Digital Fairness Fitness Check report which highlighted that some authorities are using the Commission's UCPD guidance to interpret the rules specific to online reviews, but other authorities are not.

While acknowledging that platforms hosting reviews have a responsibility for safeguarding reviews in line with existing laws as mentioned above, members nonetheless emphasise the necessity of proportionate regulation that avoids a 'one-size-fits-all' approach. From the diversity of our own members, we recognise that platforms must be able to interpret existing guidance with flexibility, and take reasonable and proportionate steps in line with the context of their own services. Despite operating within the same area, the differences between Coalition member platforms are a good illustration of the variety of platforms that are operating within the EU, which is why flexibility to apply requirements is necessary, and why considering features within the context of each platform to understand its purpose and effects, is necessary.

We thank the Commission for the opportunity to respond to its consultation on the upcoming Digital Fairness Act. Once again, we reiterate our support for the decision to not include reviews within the scope of consultation or the future DFA, believing that there is currently a sufficient and substantial legislative framework where best practice can be built upon and enforcement can be enhanced.

The Coalition is pleased to continue engaging with the Commission throughout the process and would be glad to provide industry insight into the importance of upholding trusted reviews and protecting consumers.
Members of the Coalition for Trusted Reviews are committed to the following best practices to ensure trust in online reviews and ratings:

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: Maintaining clear and transparent reviews and ratings policies that:

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

evidence_quotes.quote: Deter and seek to prohibit users from posting and soliciting fake reviews and ratings;
If applicable, explain how and when reviews and ratings (and reviewers) are verified;
Communicate to users the actions taken to detect fake reviews and ratings;

paragraphs.text: a. Detail the requirements for reviews and ratings (including who can leave the reviews and ratings and what is and is not allowed); b. Educate users about the importance of genuine and unbiased reviews; c. Deter and seek to prohibit users from posting and soliciting fake reviews and ratings; d. Explain how reviews and ratings are collected and displayed; e. If applicable, explain how and when reviews and ratings (and reviewers) are verified; f. Communicate to users the actions taken to detect fake reviews and ratings; and

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: g. Articulate, in general terms, what consequences exist when fake reviews are detected.

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: Having systems, processes and experts in place that ensure the trustworthiness of reviews and ratings, which:

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: a. continuously monitor and analyse patterns of use and behavior; b. detect and remove fake reviews; and c. have measures in place to assess the authenticity of reviewers.

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

evidence_quotes.quote: flag suspected fake reviews and ratings

paragraphs.text: Having a reporting process for users to flag suspected fake reviews and ratings, this includes:

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: a. Ensuring there are systems and teams trained to investigate reported reviews and ratings; b. Having trained people to identify and audit fake reviews missed by automated systems, and to investigate reported false positives; and c. Ongoing monitoring and auditing of the effectiveness of the automated systems, policies, the reporting of reviews and review collection.

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: Take appropriate actions on policy violations and the users (including businesses) that violate policies including:

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: a. Prompt removal of fake reviews; b. Banning, blocking, and suspending accounts that repeatedly submit or solicit fake reviews or otherwise breach reviews and ratings policies; c. Enforcement actions against individuals, businesses, or locations repeatedly linked to fake reviews; and d. Offer prominent warnings to consumers or removal of a business' listing in the event of prolonged abuses.

section_path: Annex A: The Coalition for Trusted Reviews Best Practices

paragraphs.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
